title: Decalepis khasiana
intro: Decalepis khasiana is a rare and endangered plant species belonging to the Asclepiadaceae family, native to the Western Ghats of India. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, inflammation, and respiratory disorders, due to its potential anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Decalepis khasiana include alkaloids, glycosides, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional Indian medicine, Decalepis khasiana is often used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as in the form of powders or extracts, to harness its health benefits.
taxonomy: Decalepis khasiana is a plant species that belongs to the family Apocynaceae. This family of plants is commonly known as the dogbane or oleander family. Decalepis khasiana is further classified under the genus Decalepis, which is a monotypic genus, having only one species. The classification of Decalepis khasiana is also supported by molecular phylogenetic studies, which have confirmed its position within the Apocynaceae family. Decalepis khasiana is an endemic plant species found in the Western Ghats of India.
